changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: SIGNAL: ~90 ships still crossing Hormuz per AP — blockade is selective (US/Israeli ships banned only), not total closure. Iran easing on non-aligned nations. Putin-Trump call today yielded no Ukraine breakthrough. Pakistan-Afghanistan Eid ceasefire active Mar 18-23. ME military escalation continues with IRGC announcing Gulf energy infrastructure attacks.

latest_news.headline: QatarEnergy confirms 'extensive damage' at Ras Laffan LNG — world's largest gas export plant hit by Iranian missiles
latest_news.text: QatarEnergy says Iranian missile attacks on Ras Laffan Industrial City caused 'extensive damage' to the world's largest LNG export facility. Qatar declares Iranian military and security attaches persona non grata.

latest_news.headline: Iranian drone boat attacks two oil tankers off Basra, Iraq — 1 crew killed
latest_news.text: Two oil tankers attacked by Iranian drone boat off Port of Basra, Iraq, setting them on fire. At least 1 crew member killed, 38 rescued. Major escalation in Gulf shipping attacks.

latest_news.headline: US gas prices hit $3.84/gallon national average — war-driven energy crisis deepens
latest_news.text: Gas prices surge as Iran's Hormuz blockade paralyzes crude shipments. Brent crude ~$105-110. Fed holds rates citing war uncertainty.

latest_news.headline: Gabbard: Iran 'was not rebuilding enrichment' — contradicts war justification
latest_news.text: DNI Gabbard confirms IC assessment that Iran was not rebuilding nuclear enrichment prior to war. CIA Director Ratcliffe rejects this claim. Intelligence community divided.

latest_news.headline: Iran allows more ships through Hormuz — maritime data shows traffic nearly doubled
latest_news.text: Number of ships transiting Strait of Hormuz has nearly doubled in recent days. Iran selectively enforcing blockade — bans US/Israeli ships but allows others through.

outlook: CRITICAL (Day 24, 23:04 UTC) — (1) Ras Laffan LNG plant extensively damaged by Iran — world's largest gas export facility hit; (2) Iranian drone boat attacks 2 oil tankers off Basra — 1 killed, shipping attacks escalating; (3) Qatar expels Iranian military/security attaches — diplomatic break; (4) US gas $3.84/gal, Brent ~$105-110; (5) Gabbard contradicts war justification: Iran 'not rebuilding enrichment'; (6) Hormuz easing — ship traffic nearly doubled, blockade selective not total; (7) Pakistan-Afghanistan Eid ceasefire active (Mar 18-23); (8) Israel-Lebanon: ground operations expanding, 820+ killed in Lebanon, 1M displaced; (9) Putin-Trump call yielded no Ukraine breakthrough; (10) No ceasefire signals in ME active theaters.
